raw_transcript.speaker: Maria
raw_transcript.text: Hallo, ich bin Maria. Wie kann ich dir helfen?

raw_transcript.speaker: Maria
raw_transcript.text: Hier, dein Kaffee. Frisch gebrüht und dampfend heiß. Trink ihn in Ruhe.

raw_transcript.speaker: Maria
raw_transcript.text: Bitte schön. Lass ihn dir schmecken.

reviewer_output.overall_summary: Robert's session was very brief, covering only a basic ordering exchange in a Viennese café scenario. He demonstrated a simple but functional request, though he made one article gender error. The session would benefit from more extended practice with café vocabulary and polite register phrases typical of Viennese culture.

errors.explanation: 'Kaffee' is masculine (der Kaffee), so the accusative indefinite article must be 'einen', not 'ein'.

vocabulary_highlights.english: Enjoy it (lit. let it taste good to you)
vocabulary_highlights.note: new this session — common Austrian/German café farewell phrase
used correctly by Maria — passive exposure for Robert

strengths: Used 'Ich möchte' correctly as a polite way to order, which is more appropriate than 'Ich will' in a café context
Responded naturally with 'Danke', showing appropriate conversational turn-taking
reviewer_output.next_focus: Practice masculine accusative articles (einen, den) through café ordering drills, and learn 2-3 polite Viennese café phrases such as 'Ich hätte gerne...' and 'Zahlen bitte'.

scaffold_delivered: Ich hätte gerne einen kleinen Brauner, bitte.
Einen Moment bitte — ich überlege kurz.
scaffold_avoided: Ich hätte gerne einen kleinen Brauner, bitte.
